repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem
On some non-US keyboards, like Estonian/Swedish/etc, widely used [] characters in vim are hard to type. One way to overcome this is to use langmap option to map accented characters, that are in the same place as [] on US keyboards to []. Unfortunately this does not work currently in neovim, it cannot map multi-byte characters via langmap.

## Solution
The fix iterates over characters in typeahead and key buffers, instead of bytes, in getchar.c handle_mapping(). It does not handle macros though, these tests are still failing when turned on.

Closes #2420
Closes #27776

comment_data.body: remove this and the other whitespace/comments added below
why is this necessary (non-blocking - just curious)
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -181,6 +181,7 @@ describe('input split utf sequences', function()
 
   it('can be mapped', function()
     command('inoremap ► E296BA')
+    command('set ttimeoutlen=100') -- CI test runners may cause timeout otherwise

comment_data.body:
I saw this test failing on few archs but not all. It seemed to be caused by delay between individual bytes being longer than 50ms, even though it should've been only 10ms. I did not see that issue on my dev machine so I assumed that sometimes CI machines are not able to sleep for just 10ms and take longer time.

handle_mapping() is now sensitive to that parameter, individual bytes of multibyte character now have to arrive within ttimeoutlen to be considered as a single character. I had to add it, otherwise oldtest test_spell.vim would hang. It feeds latin instead of utf-8 to nvim and if the last character, interpreted as utf-8, needs more bytes to decode, it would just spin without timeout. In test_spell.vim's case, no actual waiting for timeout to expire is taking place, calling code just sets the timedout flag immediately when it does not have any more data.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -181,6 +181,7 @@ describe('input split utf sequences', function()
 
   it('can be mapped', function()
     command('inoremap ► E296BA')
+    command('set ttimeoutlen=100') -- CI test runners may cause timeout otherwise

comment_data.body:
single-quotes are a nice hint (vimdoc convention) that it's referring to an option
```suggestion
        // Mapped by 'langmap', update the first byte
```
this is still pending, does that mean there is still potentially more (future) work to do?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -245,7 +245,12 @@ describe("'langmap'", function()
     testrecording('<M-w>', 'ello', local_setup)
     testrecording('<M-i>x', 'hllo', local_setup)
   end)
-  pending('handles multi-byte characters', function()
+  it('handles multi-byte characters', function()
+    command('set langmap=ïxödäw')
+    feed('ïlöä')
+    expect('iwww')
+  end)
+  pending('handles multi-byte characters in a macro', function()

comment_data.body: Is this really enough to exercise the edge cases? it's good if the tests are concise, i just want to double check. `getchar.c` is one of the most critical parts of vim.

comment_data.body:
I renamed existing pending "handles multi-byte..." test cases to "handles multi-byte... in a macro",  because these are actually testing only macros, but not direct input.
It looks like the issue with langmap and macros is more general and happens with single-byte characters, too. Issue #11614 seems to be about that.

comment_data.body: I'll try to improve this a little. getchar's handle_mapping()  by itself is quite well exercised by the test suite, imo. It was pretty difficult to make all tests pass.

comment_data.body: docstring that gives a hint about the key idea of this function?
This doesn't mention that it expects K_SPECIAL bytes to be escaped.

comment_regular_data.body: Pretty sure there were related issues and/or PRs for this (possibly closed). Can you reference them
I found #2420 and #27776, which seem to directly relate to that fix.
if this closes those, can you please update the PR description to mark them as closed? also mention 'langmap' in the pr title
Removed more added newlines for cleaner patch

Does this have any implications for https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/11971 ?

#6494 may be worth referencing if you plan to address the macros case.

> Does this have any implications for #11971 ?
> 
> #6494 may be worth referencing if you plan to address the macros case.

Key combinations still do not work. If I do "set langmap=ä]", then Ctrl-ä does not map to Ctrl-],  but just plain ]. It is not limited to multibyte chars, single byte chars do not work either, but in a different way than described in that issue.
For me, it's a relatively minor thing. I can remap Ctrl-ä in my terminal  app to Ctrl-]. But I cannot map ä to ] because I still need to type ä sometimes.

> I do "set langmap=ä]", then Ctrl-ä does not map to Ctrl-], but just plain ]. It is not limited to multibyte chars, single byte chars do not work either, but in a different way than described in that issue

ok, is there a docs update needed? the 'langmap' doc is [here](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/400f2473972898ecfd89ac3c55222ace0cc248dd/src/nvim/options.lua#L5090) , then just run `make doc` locally to regenerate.

also think any improvement gained by this PR is worth mentioning in news.txt, since people have wanted 'langmap' improvements for a long time.

title: fix(mappings): langmap with multibyte characters
